title: CHAPTER 4 —
DOCUMENT PREPARATION
segments.body_html:
The key idea is this: in a well-structured document, there is a strict separation between what something is and what it

Chapter 4 — Document Preparation — When you manually bold a paragraph and make it 18-point and blue, you have made it look like a heading. But the document

beats.text: The key idea is this: in a well-structured document, there is a strict separation between what something is and what it looks like. When you manually bold a paragraph and make it 18-point and blue, you have made it look like a heading. But the document has no idea it is a heading. It is just text that happens to be large and blue. The document cannot reason about it.

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

title: THE TABLE OF
CONTENTS PROBLEM.
segments.body_html:
There is a downstream consequence of labels that is worth understanding separately, because it reveals something importa

The Table of Contents Problem — A table of contents is not a cosmetic feature. It is a map of your document's structure. When you ask Word or Docs to ge

beats.text: There is a downstream consequence of labels that is worth understanding separately, because it reveals something important about what structure actually means. A table of contents is not a cosmetic feature. It is a map of your document's structure. When you ask Word or Docs to generate a table of contents automatically, what the software is actually doing is scanning the document for paragraphs labeled with heading styles , Heading 1, Heading 2, Heading 3 , and assembling them into a hierarchy with page numbers.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · THE TABLE OF CONTENTS PROBLEM

title: THEMES:
ONE LAYER UP
segments.body_html:
A theme is a coordinated set of fonts, colors, and spacing that applies to an entire document at once. In Word, themes l

Themes: One Layer Up — The way themes interact with styles is what makes them powerful. When you apply a theme, every style in the document tha

beats.text: A theme is a coordinated set of fonts, colors, and spacing that applies to an entire document at once. In Word, themes live on the Design tab. In Docs, they are under Format > Themes. The way themes interact with styles is what makes them powerful. When you apply a theme, every style in the document that uses a theme color or a theme font inherits the new values.

segments.label: 03 / 13 · THEMES: ONE LAYER UP

title: HEADERS, FOOTERS, AND
THE REPEATED INFORMATION PROBLEM
segments.body_html:
Imagine a 40-page report. The reader is on page 23. They need to know which document this is, which section they are in,

Headers, Footers, and the Repeated Infor — Headers and footers solve this by being a different layer. The main text of your document lives in the body layer. The h

beats.text: Imagine a 40-page report. The reader is on page 23. They need to know which document this is, which section they are in, and which page they are looking at. If you typed that information manually into the top or bottom of every page, you would spend twenty minutes on it , and the moment you added or deleted a page, the page numbers would all be wrong. Headers and footers solve this by being a different layer.

segments.label: 04 / 13 · HEADERS, FOOTERS, AND THE REPE

segments.section: LISTS AND WHAT THEY ARE ACTUAL
title: LISTS AND
WHAT THEY ARE ACTUALLY FOR
segments.body_html:
—
Three-Column Comparison Of Bulleted List — Three-Column Comparison Of Bulleted List — what it claims about the items app
Numbered List — Numbered List — what it claims about the items applied to the chapter example.
And Prose — — And Prose — — what it claims about the items applied to the chapter example.

beats.text: A list is not just a visual alternative to a paragraph. It is a different claim about structure. When you write a paragraph, you are claiming that the ideas in it are connected , that they belong together in the flow of a single thought. When you write a bulleted list, you are claiming the opposite: that these items are parallel, independent, and best understood by scanning rather than reading. The visual separation signals something to the reader: treat each of these items as its own unit.

segments.label: 05 / 13 · LISTS AND WHAT THEY ARE ACTUAL

title: TABLES:
WHEN STRUCTURE HAS TWO DIMENSIONS
segments.body_html:
18%
market share, charges premium prices, and has a mature produ
→
12%
market share, is mid-market priced, and is growing
→
18%
| Premium | Mature |

beats.text: A table is the right tool when your data has two dimensions , when you are comparing multiple items across multiple variables. One dimension becomes rows. The other becomes columns. The cell at their intersection holds a single value or fact. The competitive landscape of the WorldCorp market report is a good example. You have five competitors. You want to compare them on market share, pricing, and product maturity.

segments.label: 06 / 13 · TABLES: WHEN STRUCTURE HAS TWO

title: TEMPLATES:
ENCODING DECISIONS BEFORE YOU WRITE
segments.body_html:
A template is a document that contains structure but no content. It encodes your style definitions, your heading hierarc

Templates: Encoding Decisions Before You — The value of a template is that it moves decisions from the writing moment to the design moment. When you are writing, y

beats.text: A template is a document that contains structure but no content. It encodes your style definitions, your heading hierarchy, your headers and footers, your page margins, your theme , all the decisions about how a document should be organized , and then sits as a starting point for every document of that type. The value of a template is that it moves decisions from the writing moment to the design moment. When you are writing, you want to be thinking about what you are saying.

segments.label: 07 / 13 · TEMPLATES: ENCODING DECISIONS

title: THE DOCUMENT AS
A SYSTEM.
segments.body_html:
Here is the thing that all of these ideas have in common.

The Document as a System — A document, when it is built correctly, behaves like a system. Each component has a defined role. The style definition s

beats.text: Here is the thing that all of these ideas have in common. A document, when it is built correctly, behaves like a system. Each component has a defined role. The style definition says what a heading is. The heading label says which paragraphs play that role. The theme says what the role looks like in this document's visual language. The header repeats the document's identity on every page. The table of contents reads the structure and makes it navigable.

segments.label: 08 / 13 · THE DOCUMENT AS A SYSTEM

title: EXERCISES
segments.body_html: 1.→2.→3.→4.→5.

beats.text: Open a blank Word document or Google Doc. Type five lines: a title, two section headings, and two paragraphs of body text. Now apply styles , Title, Heading 1, and Normal , to the appropriate lines. Then open the Navigation Pane (Word: View > Navigation Pane; Docs: View > Show document outline). What appears? Now select a heading that you formatted manually (bolded, resized) but did not apply a style to.

segments.label: 09 / 13 · EXERCISES

title: AI WAYBACK
MACHINE.
segments.body_html:
·Ask it to explain why Scribe's enforcement of styles-over-direct-formatting was controversial in 1980, and which side of
Ask"Reid's 1980 Carnegie Mellon dissertation introduced Scribe as a system that 'imposes structure.' What's the cost of imp
Add the framing"Answer as a 1980 academic explaining to a typist why she should switch from a typewriter to Scribe"

beats.text: The ideas in this chapter didn't appear from nowhere. Brian Reid was working on Scribe, the first document-preparation system to enforce a strict separation between content and formatting , the idea behind every "use styles, not direct formatting" rule in modern Word decades before most people had heard of document styles and templates. Here's a prompt to find out more , and then make it better.

segments.label: 10 / 13 · AI WAYBACK MACHINE

The figure should represent: two-column split showing "manual formatting" path (change one heading goes to edit more goes to sections move goes to headings drift) vs. "styles" path (define role once goes to modify style goes to all instances update).
